text:
Deep learning has spread to a wide range of fields from grid-based data like images to relational structures in the form of Graph Neural Networks (GNNs). This survey paper attempts to summarize all the basic concepts, structures and algorithms that are used in GNNs to explore how to deal with the complex relationships between the nodes and edges in graphs. GNNs can recognize specific entities and relations in various domains such as texts and social networks, and they learn to pass messages on non-Euclidean graphs through the exploration of message passing.

This review focuses on the state-of-the-art graph neural network (GNN) models including Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs), GraphSAGE, and Graph Attention Networks (GATs) and compares their characteristics and performance. In addition, this paper presents a taxonomy of graph structures (i.e., directed graphs, heterogeneous graphs, and knowledge graphs) and different learning tasks including node classification, graph classification, and link prediction. It also reviews the applications of GNNs in natural language processing, computer vision, and bioinformatics, as well as the popular datasets, tools, and challenges in this area. Finally, the challenges and potential research avenues are concluded including dealing with unbalanced datasets and improving the structural analysis.

prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
